programmes.show_description: (2021) Ryûsuke Hamaguchi's triptych of short dramas tells the stories of three different women caught up in very different romantic relationships. In Japanese/subs.
(1976) Touching romance starring Sean Connery and Audrey Hepburn. The ageing legend Robin Hood returns to Sherwood Forest and discovers that, while he was away, Marian has become an Abbess.
Tom Hanks and director Paul Greengrass talk about making the tense, real-life thriller Captain Phillips.
(1964) Adventure with Richard Widmark as a Viking who leads his men on the quest for a huge bell made of solid gold, but with Sidney Poitier, leader of an army of Moors, on the trail too.
Tom Hanks and director Paul Greengrass talk about making the tense, real-life thriller Captain Phillips.
(1971) Sci-fi thriller. When a space rocket crash-lands in the US, a fatal alien virus is released, sparking a desperate battle to defeat the extraterrestrial life form and save mankind.
(2019) Visually stunning sci-fi starring Brad Pitt as a famously cool astronaut sent to the edges of the solar system to save Earth from deadly power surges. With Tommy Lee Jones.
(2019) Wickedly funny comedy horror starring Samara Weaving. A bride finds herself being hunted down by her new in-laws when she's asked to observe a deadly family tradition.
(2022) Alex Garland's graphic, gory horror with Jessie Buckley and Rory Kinnear. A traumatised woman tries to find solace in the English countryside only to encounter some very odd men.
(1982) Sidney J Furie's seminal horror stars Barbara Hershey as a divorced mother of three who becomes the victim of a poltergeist that submits her to multiple violent and sexual assaults.
(1941) Walter Pidgeon, Maureen O'Hara and Roddy McDowall star in John Ford's multi-Oscar-winning period drama set in the mining communities of the South Wales Valleys of the 19th century.
(1959) Comedy western with Kenneth More. An English gunsmith decides the Wild West is the best market for his wares, but little does he expect to end up sheriff of a lawless frontier town.
(1956) Western. Richard Widmark plays a man who murdered the killers of his wife and children and who becomes the unlikely saviour of a wagon train that's attacked by Apaches.
(1962) War drama. Darryl F Zanuck's classic Oscar-winning recreation of the Allied landings in Normandy on 6 June 1944, with an all-star British, American, German and French cast.
(2010) Action-comedy starring Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz. A CIA assassin kidnaps a woman and takes her on an impromptu round-the-world trip in order to save both their lives. Violence.
